source.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: But because you have desired from me in all my letters which I had sent to you precepts to nourish your life and faith, it has come to pass that, through the frequency of my writings to you, I have now exhausted language of that kind; and I can really write nothing new to you, so as to avoid what I have written before.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: And in truth, through the goodness of God, you do not now need to be exhorted, inasmuch as, perfecting your faith at the very beginning of your saintly life, you display a devoted love in Christ.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: One thing, however, I do press upon you, that you do not go back on things you have already passed away from, that you do not long again for things you have already scorned, and that, having put your hand to the plow, you do not look back again, retracing your steps; for, undoubtedly, by falling into this fault, your furrow will lose its straightness, and the cultivator will not receive his own proper reward.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: Moreover, he does not secure even a measure of the reward, if he has, in a measure, failed.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: For, as we must flee from sin to righteousness, so he who has entered on the practice of righteousness must beware lest he lay himself open to sin.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: For it is written that “his righteousness shall not profit the righteous on the day on which he has gone astray.”

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: For this, then, we must take our stand, for this we must labor, that we, who have escaped from sins, do not lose the prepared rewards.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: For the enemy stands ready against us, that he may at once strike the man who has been stripped of the shield of faith.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: Our shield, therefore, is not to be cast aside, lest our side be exposed to attack; and our sword is not to be put away, lest the enemy then begin to give up all fear: moreover, we know that if he sees a man fully armed, he will retreat.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: Nor are we ignorant that it is a hard and difficult thing daily to fight against the flesh and the world.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: But if you reflect upon eternity, and if you consider the kingdom of heaven, which undoubtedly the Lord will condescend to bestow upon us although we are sinners, what suffering, I ask, is sufficiently great, by which we may merit such things?

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: And besides, our struggle in this world is but for a short time; for although death do not speedily overtake us, old age will come.

verses.book: Letter I. A Letter of the Holy Presbyter Severus to His Sister Claudia Concerning the Last Judgment.
verses.text: The years flow on, and time glides by; while, as I hope, the Lord Jesus will speedily call us to himself, as being dear to his heart.
